content: Expectation 3 of the ECB Guide requires that the management body considers climate-related and environmental risks when developing the institution's overall business strategy, business objectives and risk management framework, and exercises effective oversight. This means C&E risks should be factored into strategic planning processes, including decisions about which markets to operate in, which sectors to grow or reduce exposure to, and how to allocate capital. The ECB expects institutions to assess the impact of C&E risks on their current business model and to consider the need for adjustments over different time horizons. This includes developing a credible transition plan that outlines how the institution will adapt to a lower-carbon economy. Leading institutions set quantified green financing or sustainable lending targets, integrate climate considerations into product development, and align their capital allocation with the goals of the Paris Agreement. The management body — both in its management and supervisory functions — is expected to have sufficient knowledge of C&E risks to provide effective strategic direction and challenge.
